title: Muninn source metadata (sources/<id>/meta.yml)
description: The frontmatter contract of an immutable captured source (SPEC §5.1, format 1.0). Sources are captured once and never edited in place (I1); changed content makes a new version recorded in the history ledger (§4.3). Unknown fields are permitted — forward compatibility is part of the format (§5). What this schema cannot check — hash correctness against bytes on disk (L5), ledger ↔ disk coverage (L13), every-source-has-a-summary (L15) — is enforced by the linter.

id.description: Stable for the life of the source; referenced by provenance and links (§4.1). Recommended form: src-<slug>.

system.description: e.g. file, url, chat, repo, jira, api, …

ref.description: Canonical locator within the system. Also the deterministic identity signal of the T-045 locator rung — give each distinct evolving source a distinct ref.

recoverable.description: True when the original bytes can be re-fetched via ref (ADR-0010); false for e.g. chat (ADR-0003).

capture_reason.description: Why the source is held as a reference, not in full — required iff capture: reference.

history.description: The version ledger (§4.3) — one entry per version, prior bytes retained on disk, never overwritten.

file.description: Canonical bytes: source.<ext> for the current version, source.v<N>.<ext> for prior.

text_aid.description: The non-authoritative extracted-text aid for this version, if any (ADR-0010).

extracted_by.description: Which extractor produced the aid (present iff text_aid).

supersedes.description: Version number this replaced, or null for v1.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical captured content (§4.2): raw bytes for a binary source; the LF-normalized, frontmatter-stripped body for a text source.
